mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, directly boosting S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises above the 0.2pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand expansion, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint positive growth condition yields a negative or zero mean 60-day forward return over any 24-month rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the industrial production index during a monetary tightening cycle reflects supply-side constraints and rising input costs, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive or flat when INDPRO declines during periods where FEDFUNDS increases by more than 0.25% over the prior quarter.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPIAUCSL reading above 2.8 triggers expectations of restrictive monetary policy, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in over 60% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 2.8 threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls expands aggregate income and corporate revenue expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Rolling 60-day forward returns show a negative or statistically insignificant correlation with PAYEMS growth over any 36-month window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent quarterly expansion in real GDP and monthly retail sales confirms robust domestic demand, prompting immediate positive S&P 500 repricing over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined positive growth signal fails to produce a mean 5-day forward return greater than zero across 10 consecutive quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in industrial production during a late-cycle expansion indicate capacity utilization peaks and impending supply bottlenecks, causing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive when INDPRO declines during periods where GDPC1 growth exceeds 2.5% annually.
